title: Heatwave Alert: Odisha To Burn For 5 More Days! Jharsuguda Boils At 45.2 Degrees - Kanak News Odisha
full_text:
Odisha is literally boiling under a merciless summer heatwave with temperatures shattering records! Western Odisha is currently the worst-hit zone as mercury levels cross an unbearable 45 degrees Celsius.
The regional meteorological department has warned that Odisha will continue to burn for the next 5 days. Today, 11 cities in Western Odisha recorded temperatures above 42°C, with 5 cities crossing the dangerous 45°C mark. Jharsuguda has emerged as the hottest city at a blistering 45.2°C. While Western Odisha suffers from scorching dry heat, coastal Odisha is set to choke under extreme humidity and sultry conditions. Watch this special weather report for the latest IMD warnings, city-wise temperatures, and heatwave safety tips.

title: Massive Heatwave in Odisha: Streets Deserted, 23 Districts Under Yellow Warning! - Kanak News Odisha
full_text:
Odisha is burning! The mercury is hitting record-breaking levels, turning Western Odisha into a furnace.
With temperatures soaring past 44°C in Jharsuguda, Sambalpur, and Hirakud, the state is reeling under an intense heatwave. By 2:30 PM, the heat became unbearable, and the humidity in coastal Odisha is making life even more miserable for residents. Streets are wearing a deserted look as if under an undeclared curfew by 8 AM.
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued a stern warning: No relief is expected for the next 6 to 7 days. An Orange Warning has been issued for Jharsuguda, Sambalpur, and Bolangir, while 23 other districts are under a Yellow Alert. Stay safe, stay hydrated, and avoid stepping out unless absolutely necessary.
